topics.desc: Set your name globally for all commits
Set your email globally for all commits
View all current Git configuration settings
Initialize a new Git repository in the current directory
Clone a remote repository to your local machine
Show the working tree status — staged, unstaged and untracked files
Stage a specific file or all changes for the next commit
topics.code:
git add file.txt
git add .          // stage everything

topics.desc: Commit staged changes with a message
Modify the most recent commit message or add forgotten changes
Show unstaged changes. Use --staged to see staged changes
Temporarily save uncommitted changes without committing
topics.code:
git stash
git stash pop      // restore latest stash
git stash list     // view all stashes

topics.desc: List all local branches. Add -a to include remote branches
topics.code:
git branch
git branch -a      // all branches

topics.desc: Create a new branch without switching to it
Create and immediately switch to a new branch
Modern way to switch branches (Git 2.23+)
topics.code:
git switch main
git switch -c feature/login   // create + switch

topics.desc: Delete a branch. Use -D to force delete unmerged branches
topics.code:
git branch -d feature/login
git branch -D feature/login   // force

topics.desc: Rename the current branch
Merge another branch into the current branch
Merge with a merge commit even if fast-forward is possible
Reapply commits on top of another branch for a linear history
Interactive rebase — squash, reorder, edit or drop commits
topics.code: git rebase -i HEAD~3   // edit last 3 commits

topics.desc: Apply a specific commit from another branch to the current branch
After fixing conflicts manually, stage the files and complete the merge
View all configured remote connections
Add a new remote connection with a name
Download changes from remote but don't merge into local branch
Fetch and merge remote changes into the current branch
topics.code:
git pull origin main
git pull --rebase origin main   // rebase instead of merge

topics.desc: Push local commits to the remote repository
topics.code:
git push origin main
git push -u origin feature/login  // set upstream

topics.desc: Safer force push — fails if someone else has pushed since your last fetch
Discard unstaged changes in a file (replaces old git checkout -- file)
topics.code:
git restore file.txt
git restore .         // discard all unstaged

topics.desc: Unstage a file without discarding changes
Create a new commit that undoes a specific commit — safe for shared branches
Undo commits but keep changes staged
topics.code: git reset --soft HEAD~1   // undo last commit, keep staged

topics.desc: Undo commits and unstage changes but keep files (default reset)
topics.code: git reset HEAD~1          // undo + unstage, keep files

topics.desc: Undo commits and permanently discard all changes — destructive!
topics.code: git reset --hard HEAD~1   // ⚠️ changes are lost

topics.desc: Remove untracked files. Use -fd to also remove untracked directories
topics.code: git clean -fd             // remove untracked files + dirs

topics.desc: Show commit history with full details
Compact one-line-per-commit history view
topics.code:
git log --oneline
git log --oneline --graph --all   // visual branch tree

topics.desc: Filter commit history by author name
Show full diff for each commit in the log
topics.code: git log -p -2   // last 2 commits with diff

topics.desc: Show details and diff of a specific commit
Show who last modified each line of a file
Show all HEAD movements — useful for recovering lost commits
List all existing tags
Create a lightweight tag at the current commit
Create an annotated tag with a message — preferred for releases
Push all local tags to the remote repository
Delete a local tag
topics.code:
git tag -d v1.0.0
git push origin --delete v1.0.0   // delete remote tag

topics.desc: Save uncommitted changes with a descriptive name
List all saved stashes
Apply the most recent stash and remove it from the stash list
Apply a specific stash without removing it from the stash list
Delete a specific stash entry
Remove all stash entries permanently
Binary search through commits to find which one introduced a bug
topics.code:
git bisect start
git bisect bad          // current commit is broken
git bisect good v1.0    // this tag was working
// Git checks out commits for you to test
git bisect reset        // done

topics.desc: Check out multiple branches simultaneously in separate directories
Include another Git repository as a subdirectory of your project
topics.code:
git submodule add https://github.com/user/lib.git libs/lib
git submodule update --init --recursive

topics.desc: Summarize commit history grouped by author
topics.code: git shortlog -sn   // sorted by number of commits

topics.desc: List files and patterns Git should not track
Stop tracking a file that is already committed without deleting it
topics.code:
git rm --cached file.txt
git rm -r --cached build/

topics.desc: Set a global gitignore file for all repos on your machine
Small focused commits are easier to review, revert and understand than large ones
Prefix commits with a type for readable history and changelog generation
topics.code:
feat: add dark mode toggle
fix: crash on empty list
chore: update dependencies
refactor: extract LoginViewModel
docs: update README

topics.desc: Force pushing to shared branches rewrites history for everyone. Use --force-with-lease on feature branches only
Fetch the latest changes before pushing to avoid conflicts
topics.code:
git pull --rebase origin main
git push origin main

topics.desc: Keep main/master clean. Do all work on feature branches and merge via PRs
